input: How much is the entrance fee at Puraran Beach?
output: Puraran Beach charges around PHP 50 per person as an environmental fee. Surfboard rental typically costs PHP 200–350 per hour, and surf lessons with a local instructor range from PHP 500–800 per session.
title: Puraran Beach Entrance Fee
summary_offline: Puraran Beach entrance is around PHP 50 environmental fee. Surfboard rental PHP 200–350/hour, surf lessons PHP 500–800/session.

input: What is the entrance fee at Binurong Point?
output: Binurong Point currently has no official entrance fee. However, a minimal community donation of PHP 20–50 is appreciated. Hiring a habal-habal (motorcycle) from Gigmoto proper to the viewpoint costs around PHP 100–150 per person.
title: Binurong Point Entrance Fee
summary_offline: No entrance fee at Binurong Point. Community donation of PHP 20–50 appreciated. Habal-habal from Gigmoto costs PHP 100–150.

input: How much does it cost to visit Twin Rock Beach Resort for a day?
output: Twin Rock Beach Resort charges a day-use fee of around PHP 150–200 per person, which typically includes access to the beach and pool area. Overnight cottage and room rates range from PHP 1,500–4,000 depending on the accommodation type.
title: Twin Rock Beach Resort Day Use Fee
summary_offline: Twin Rock day use fee around PHP 150–200/person. Overnight rooms PHP 1,500–4,000.

input: Is there an entrance fee at Maribina Falls?
output: Maribina Falls charges a small entrance fee of about PHP 30–50 per person. A local guide is recommended and costs around PHP 150–250 for the trek, which takes 30–45 minutes each way through the forest.
title: Maribina Falls Entrance Fee
summary_offline: Maribina Falls entrance fee is PHP 30–50. Local guide PHP 150–250. Trek is 30–45 minutes each way.

input: What is the entrance fee at Nahulugan Falls?
output: Nahulugan Falls has an entrance fee of approximately PHP 50 per person. Visitors are advised to hire a local guide for around PHP 200 as the trail can be slippery. The natural pool at the base is free to swim in.
title: Nahulugan Falls Entrance Fee
summary_offline: Nahulugan Falls entrance is PHP 50. Guide recommended at PHP 200. Natural pool is free to swim.

input: How much is the entrance fee at Tuwad-Tuwadan Blue Lagoon?
output: Tuwad-Tuwadan Blue Lagoon charges around PHP 30–50 per person. It is one of the more affordable day trips from the Pandan or Caramoran area and offers snorkeling and swimming in crystal-clear waters.
title: Tuwad-Tuwadan Blue Lagoon Fee
summary_offline: Tuwad-Tuwadan Blue Lagoon entrance is PHP 30–50 per person. Good for swimming and snorkeling.

input: Is there an entrance fee at Luyang Cave?
output: Luyang Cave charges an entrance fee of around PHP 50–100 per person. A mandatory guide fee of PHP 150–200 is also collected at the barangay. Bring your own flashlight or headlamp as the cave interior is dark.
title: Luyang Cave Entrance Fee
summary_offline: Luyang Cave entrance PHP 50–100 plus guide fee PHP 150–200. Bring a flashlight.

input: Is Museo De Catanduanes free?
output: Museo De Catanduanes is free to enter, though a voluntary donation is appreciated. The museum is government-managed and showcases the history, culture, and natural heritage of Catanduanes. No reservation is needed.
title: Museo De Catanduanes Entrance Fee
summary_offline: Museo De Catanduanes is free. Voluntary donations accepted. No reservation needed.

input: How much does it cost to visit Macutal Falls?
output: Visiting Macutal Falls involves a guide fee of PHP 250–350 as the trail requires local knowledge. There may be a minimal barangay fee of PHP 30–50. The trek takes about 45 minutes to 1 hour from the jump-off point.
title: Macutal Falls Fees
summary_offline: Macutal Falls guide fee PHP 250–350. Barangay fee around PHP 30–50. Trek is 45 mins to 1 hour.

input: How much does a boat trip to Palumbanes Island cost?
output: A boat charter to Palumbanes Island from the nearest jump-off typically costs PHP 600–1,200 per boat depending on the season and negotiation. Splitting the cost among a group of 4–6 makes it very affordable. Bring your own food and water as there are no vendors on the island.
title: Palumbanes Island Boat Trip Cost
summary_offline: Boat charter to Palumbanes Island costs PHP 600–1,200 per boat. Bring food and water, no vendors on island.

input: What is the entrance fee at Mamangal Beach?
output: Mamangal Beach is a free public beach with no entrance fee. It is one of the most popular and accessible beaches near Virac. Facilities are basic, so bring your own supplies. Nearby food stalls sell local snacks for around PHP 30–100.
title: Mamangal Beach Entrance Fee
summary_offline: Mamangal Beach is free. Basic facilities, bring your own supplies. Nearby snacks from PHP 30–100.

input: How much is the entrance to Hinagasan Falls?
output: Hinagasan Falls has a minimal entrance fee of around PHP 30–50 per person collected by the local barangay. The falls are accessible without a guide, though the trail can be slippery after rain.
title: Hinagasan Falls Entrance Fee
summary_offline: Hinagasan Falls entrance fee PHP 30–50. Accessible without guide but trail can be slippery.

input: How much is the entrance fee at Cagnipa Rolling Hills?
output: Cagnipa Rolling Hills charges around PHP 50 per person as an entrance or environmental fee. The viewpoint offers one of the best panoramic views of the northern tip of Catanduanes and is well worth the small fee.
title: Cagnipa Rolling Hills Fee
summary_offline: Cagnipa Rolling Hills entrance fee PHP 50. Panoramic view of northern Catanduanes.

input: What is the entrance fee at Campilan Beach?
output: Campilan Beach is a free public beach in Caramoran. No entrance fee is collected. The beach is known for its white sand and calm waters, making it ideal for a budget-friendly swim.
title: Campilan Beach Entrance Fee
summary_offline: Campilan Beach is free. White sand, calm water. No entrance fee.

input: How do I get from Virac to Puraran Beach?
output: From Virac to Puraran Beach, you can take a van or jeepney bound for Baras at the Virac terminal. The ride takes approximately 1.5 to 2 hours. From Baras proper, hire a habal-habal (motorcycle taxi) for the last stretch to Puraran. You can also rent a multicab or van for hire for around PHP 1,500–2,500 for the whole vehicle.
title: Route from Virac to Puraran Beach
summary_offline: Virac to Puraran: van/jeepney to Baras (1.5–2 hrs) then habal-habal. Van for hire PHP 1,500–2,500.

input: How do I get to Binurong Point from Virac?
output: From Virac, take a van or bus heading north toward Gigmoto. The trip takes about 2 to 3 hours depending on stops. From Gigmoto town proper, hire a habal-habal for the climb up to Binurong Point viewpoint, costing around PHP 100–150 per person. Vans for hire from Virac to Gigmoto cost PHP 2,000–3,000.
title: Route from Virac to Binurong Point
summary_offline: Virac to Binurong Point: van/bus north to Gigmoto (2–3 hrs), then habal-habal PHP 100–150. Van for hire PHP 2,000–3,000.

input: How do I get to Twin Rock Beach Resort from Virac?
output: Twin Rock Beach Resort is located in Bgy. Magnesia del Norte, Virac, about 30–40 minutes from the town center. You can take a tricycle or habal-habal from Virac for around PHP 80–150, or rent a vehicle. The resort is well-signposted from the main road.
title: Route from Virac to Twin Rock Beach Resort
summary_offline: Twin Rock is 30–40 mins from Virac town. Tricycle or habal-habal PHP 80–150.

input: How do I get to Maribina Falls from Virac?
output: From Virac, take a tricycle or habal-habal toward Cavinitan or San Jose. Maribina Falls is roughly 45 minutes from Virac proper. Ask drivers for 'Maribina' — most locals know it. The tricycle fare is around PHP 50–80 one-way.
title: Route from Virac to Maribina Falls
summary_offline: Virac to Maribina Falls: tricycle or habal-habal, 45 mins, PHP 50–80 one-way.

input: How do I reach Nahulugan Falls from Virac?
output: Nahulugan Falls is located in Bato municipality. From Virac, take a van or bus toward Bato (about 1 hour). From Bato town proper, hire a habal-habal to the falls jump-off point for PHP 100–150. Local guides can be arranged at the barangay hall.
title: Route from Virac to Nahulugan Falls
summary_offline: Virac to Nahulugan Falls: van/bus to Bato (1 hr), then habal-habal PHP 100–150.

input: How do I get from Virac to Tuwad-Tuwadan Blue Lagoon?
output: Tuwad-Tuwadan Blue Lagoon is in the northern part of Catanduanes near Caramoran. From Virac, take a van or bus heading north and alight at the nearest junction in Caramoran (about 2.5–3 hours). A habal-habal from Caramoran proper to the lagoon costs around PHP 100–200.
title: Route from Virac to Tuwad-Tuwadan Blue Lagoon
summary_offline: Virac to Tuwad-Tuwadan: van north to Caramoran (2.5–3 hrs), then habal-habal PHP 100–200.

input: How do I get to Luyang Cave from Virac?
output: Luyang Cave is in the western part of Catanduanes near San Andres or Bato. From Virac, take a bus or van heading toward San Andres (about 1–1.5 hours). Ask to be dropped off near the Luyang Cave junction, then hire a habal-habal for the last stretch.
title: Route from Virac to Luyang Cave
summary_offline: Virac to Luyang Cave: bus/van to San Andres direction (1–1.5 hrs), then habal-habal.

input: How do I get from Virac to Mamangal Beach?
output: Mamangal Beach is located south of Virac town and is accessible by tricycle from the town center. The ride takes about 40–50 minutes and costs PHP 60–100 one-way. It is one of the easier beaches to reach without renting a private vehicle.
title: Route from Virac to Mamangal Beach
summary_offline: Mamangal Beach is 40–50 mins from Virac by tricycle, PHP 60–100 one-way.

input: How do I get to Macutal Falls from Virac?
output: Macutal Falls is in the Gigmoto area. From Virac, take a north-bound van toward Gigmoto (about 2–2.5 hours). From Gigmoto, hire a habal-habal or ask a guide to lead you to the trailhead. It is best to arrange a guide beforehand through your accommodation.
title: Route from Virac to Macutal Falls
summary_offline: Virac to Macutal Falls: van to Gigmoto (2–2.5 hrs), then habal-habal or guide to trailhead.

input: How do I get to Cagnipa Rolling Hills from Virac?
output: Cagnipa Rolling Hills is near Caramoran in the north. From Virac, take a van or bus heading north to Caramoran (around 2.5–3 hours). From Caramoran town, take a habal-habal toward Cagnipa for PHP 80–120. The rolling hills are visible from the road on clear days.
title: Route from Virac to Cagnipa Rolling Hills
summary_offline: Virac to Cagnipa Rolling Hills: van north to Caramoran (2.5–3 hrs), then habal-habal PHP 80–120.

input: How do I get to Campilan Beach from Virac?
output: Campilan Beach is in Caramoran, roughly 2.5–3 hours north of Virac. Take a northbound van or bus from the Virac terminal. The beach is close to Caramoran town proper — a short tricycle or habal-habal ride from the drop-off point.
title: Route from Virac to Campilan Beach
summary_offline: Virac to Campilan Beach: van/bus north to Caramoran (2.5–3 hrs), short tricycle ride to beach.

input: How do I get to Museo De Catanduanes from Virac Airport?
output: Museo De Catanduanes is located near the Virac town center, about 1.5–2 km from the airport. From the airport, take a tricycle (PHP 30–50) or walk in about 20 minutes. It is on the main road heading into town, easy to find.
title: Route from Virac Airport to Museo De Catanduanes
summary_offline: Museo De Catanduanes is 1.5–2 km from Virac Airport. Tricycle PHP 30–50 or 20-min walk.

input: How do I get from the Virac port to the town center?
output: The Virac port (San Andres Wharf or Virac Wharf) is a short distance from the town center. Tricycles are available at the port exit for around PHP 20–40. The ride takes about 5–10 minutes. On foot it is roughly a 10–15 minute walk.
title: Route from Virac Port to Town Center
summary_offline: Virac port to town center: tricycle PHP 20–40, 5–10 mins. Walk is 10–15 mins.

input: How do I get to Hinagasan Falls from Virac?
output: Hinagasan Falls is accessible from Virac by taking a tricycle or habal-habal toward Barangay Cavinitan or Hiyop in Virac. The journey is around 30–40 minutes from town. Local tricycle drivers know the route and charge PHP 50–80.
title: Route from Virac to Hinagasan Falls
summary_offline: Virac to Hinagasan Falls: tricycle or habal-habal, 30–40 mins, PHP 50–80.

input: How do I get from Virac to Palumbanes Island?
output: To reach Palumbanes Island, travel from Virac to the western coast near San Andres or Bato municipality (about 1–1.5 hours by van). From the nearest coastal barangay, arrange a boat with local fishermen — a charter typically costs PHP 600–1,200 for the whole boat depending on the departure point. The boat ride takes about 20–40 minutes.
title: Route from Virac to Palumbanes Island
summary_offline: Virac to Palumbanes Island: van to western coast (1–1.5 hrs), then boat charter PHP 600–1,200.

input: What are the operating hours of Museo De Catanduanes?
output: Museo De Catanduanes is open Monday to Friday from 8:00 AM to 5:00 PM. It is closed on weekends and public holidays. Entry is free and no advance reservation is required. Best visited in the morning when it is less crowded.
title: Museo De Catanduanes Hours
summary_offline: Museo De Catanduanes open Mon–Fri, 8 AM–5 PM. Closed weekends and holidays.

input: What time does Puraran Beach open?
output: Puraran Beach is a natural beach and is accessible any time, though most tourism activity runs from 6:00 AM to 6:00 PM. Surf shops and rental operators typically open from 7:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Arriving early in the morning ensures the best surf conditions and fewer crowds.
title: Puraran Beach Hours
summary_offline: Puraran Beach accessible all day. Surf shops open 7 AM–5 PM. Best surfing in the morning.

input: What are the operating hours of Twin Rock Beach Resort?
output: Twin Rock Beach Resort is generally open daily from 7:00 AM to 10:00 PM for day use visitors. Overnight guests have 24-hour access. The restaurant on site serves breakfast from 7:00 AM and closes after dinner around 9:00–10:00 PM.
title: Twin Rock Beach Resort Hours
summary_offline: Twin Rock Resort open daily 7 AM–10 PM for day use. Overnight guests have 24-hr access.

input: What time does Virac Public Market open and close?
output: Virac Public Market opens as early as 4:00 AM for fish and produce vendors, with the market in full swing from 5:00 AM to 9:00 AM. Most stalls close by noon. Some dry goods vendors operate until 5:00–6:00 PM. It is busiest in the early morning.
title: Virac Public Market Hours
summary_offline: Virac Public Market open from 4–5 AM, busiest until 9 AM. Most stalls close by noon.

input: What time does Maribina Falls close?
output: Maribina Falls is accessible from sunrise to sunset, roughly 6:00 AM to 5:00 PM. The barangay may restrict entry after 4:00 PM to ensure visitors can return before dark. It is not advisable to visit after rain due to slippery trails.
title: Maribina Falls Hours
summary_offline: Maribina Falls open 6 AM–5 PM. Last entry around 4 PM. Avoid after heavy rain.

input: When is the best time to visit Binurong Point?
output: Binurong Point is accessible any time of day, but the best time to visit is early morning between 6:00 AM and 9:00 AM for clear views before clouds roll in. The viewpoint has no strict closing time, but it is safer to leave before dusk. Most organized tours depart from Gigmoto by 7:00 AM.
title: Binurong Point Best Time to Visit
summary_offline: Binurong Point best visited 6–9 AM for clear views. No strict closing time but leave before dusk.

input: What are the hours of Luyang Cave?
output: Luyang Cave is accessible from approximately 7:00 AM to 4:00 PM daily. Guides are usually available at the barangay hall during these hours. Visiting after 4:00 PM is discouraged due to limited visibility and safety concerns inside the cave.
title: Luyang Cave Hours
summary_offline: Luyang Cave open 7 AM–4 PM. Guides available at barangay hall. No visits after 4 PM.

input: What are the opening hours of Nahulugan Falls?
output: Nahulugan Falls is open to visitors from 6:00 AM to 5:00 PM. The last entry is typically at 3:00 PM to allow enough time for the trek and return before sunset. The barangay guide post operates during these hours.
title: Nahulugan Falls Hours
summary_offline: Nahulugan Falls open 6 AM–5 PM. Last entry 3 PM for safe return before sunset.

input: What time does Cagnipa Rolling Hills open?
output: Cagnipa Rolling Hills has no strict gate hours and is accessible from dawn to dusk. Sunrise visits (around 5:30–6:30 AM) are especially popular for the golden light over the hills. The area is generally safe during daylight hours.
title: Cagnipa Rolling Hills Hours
summary_offline: Cagnipa Rolling Hills accessible dawn to dusk. Sunrise visits 5:30–6:30 AM are most popular.

input: What are the opening hours of Tuwad-Tuwadan Blue Lagoon?
output: Tuwad-Tuwadan Blue Lagoon is open daily from around 6:00 AM to 5:00 PM. It is best visited in the morning when the water is calmest and most vibrant. There is no enforced closing gate but visitors are advised to leave before dark.
title: Tuwad-Tuwadan Blue Lagoon Hours
summary_offline: Tuwad-Tuwadan Blue Lagoon open 6 AM–5 PM. Best in the morning. Leave before dark.

input: What time do surf shops at Puraran open?
output: Surf shops and instructors at Puraran Beach typically open from 7:00 AM to 5:00 PM. Board rentals are available throughout the day. Surf lessons are usually conducted in the morning from 7:00 AM to 11:00 AM when waves are more consistent.
title: Puraran Beach Surf Shop Hours
summary_offline: Puraran surf shops open 7 AM–5 PM. Lessons offered 7–11 AM. Board rentals all day.

input: What time does the Virac port operate ferries?
output: The Virac port typically has ferry departures to Tabaco City, Albay in the early morning from 5:00 AM to 7:00 AM. Return trips from Tabaco to Virac depart in the afternoon around 12:00 PM to 2:00 PM. Schedules may vary and are weather-dependent, so confirm with the shipping lines directly.
title: Virac Port Ferry Hours
summary_offline: Virac port ferries to Tabaco depart 5–7 AM. Return from Tabaco around 12–2 PM. Weather-dependent.

input: What are the operating hours of Virac Airport?
output: Virac Airport (VRC) handles flights daily, typically from around 6:00 AM to 6:00 PM. Cebu Pacific and Philippine Airlines operate the Virac-Manila route. Always check current flight schedules as they change seasonally. The airport terminal is small but has a waiting area and basic food stalls.
title: Virac Airport Operating Hours
summary_offline: Virac Airport open approx 6 AM–6 PM. CebPac and PAL serve Virac-Manila route. Check current schedules.

input: What is a habal-habal and how do I use it in Catanduanes?
output: A habal-habal is a motorcycle taxi used widely in Catanduanes for reaching places that regular vehicles cannot access — waterfalls, viewpoints, and remote beaches. You ride pillion behind the driver. Negotiate the fare before boarding; typical rates are PHP 50–200 depending on distance. Always hold on firmly and wear closed shoes.
title: Habal-Habal in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Habal-habal is a motorcycle taxi for remote areas. Fare PHP 50–200. Negotiate before boarding.

input: What types of transportation are available in Catanduanes?
output: Catanduanes has four main modes of land transport: tricycles for short trips within town (PHP 10–50), habal-habal (motorcycle taxis) for rural and inter-barangay routes (PHP 50–200), multicabs (shared minivans) for inter-municipality routes (PHP 50–150), and vans for hire (PHP 1,500–3,500 per trip for the whole vehicle). Boats and bancas are used for island-hopping and coastal access.
title: Transportation Options in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Catanduanes transport: tricycles (in-town), habal-habal (rural), multicabs (inter-municipality), vans for hire. Boats for island trips.

input: Can I rent a motorcycle in Catanduanes?
output: Yes, motorcycle rentals are available in Virac for around PHP 500–800 per day. Some guesthouses and travel shops in Virac town center offer this service. A valid driver's license is required. Renting a motorcycle is a popular option for visitors who want to explore the island at their own pace.
title: Motorcycle Rental in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Motorcycle rental in Virac costs PHP 500–800/day. License required. Available at some guesthouses.

input: Can I rent a car or van in Catanduanes?
output: Van rentals and multicab hires are available through guesthouses, travel agencies, or driver contacts in Virac. A full-day van hire (with driver) costs PHP 2,500–4,500 depending on destination and negotiation. This is ideal for groups going to multiple spots in one day.
title: Van Hire in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Van hire with driver PHP 2,500–4,500/day. Available through guesthouses and travel agencies in Virac.

input: How do I get from Manila to Catanduanes?
output: The two main options are: (1) By air — fly from Manila (NAIA) to Virac Airport (VRC) with Cebu Pacific or Philippine Airlines. Flight time is about 1 hour. (2) By sea — take a bus from Manila to Tabaco City, Albay, then a ferry from Tabaco Port to Virac Port (about 3–4 hours). The ferry trip takes approximately 2–3 hours depending on the vessel.
title: How to Get to Catanduanes from Manila
summary_offline: Manila to Catanduanes: fly to Virac Airport (1 hr) or bus to Tabaco then ferry (3–4 hrs total).

input: How long is the ferry ride from Tabaco to Virac?
output: The ferry ride from Tabaco City port (Albay) to Virac port typically takes 2 to 3 hours depending on sea conditions and the type of vessel. Faster RORO ferries may take under 2 hours. Ticket prices range from PHP 200–500 per person.
title: Tabaco to Virac Ferry Duration
summary_offline: Tabaco to Virac ferry is 2–3 hours. Ticket PHP 200–500. Faster vessels under 2 hours.

input: What is the best time of year to visit Catanduanes?
output: The best time to visit Catanduanes is during the dry season from March to June, when skies are clear and seas are calm. July to October is typhoon season — Catanduanes is one of the most typhoon-prone provinces in the Philippines, so travel during these months carries risk. Surfers specifically come for the big swells from August to December.
title: Best Time to Visit Catanduanes
summary_offline: Best time to visit: March–June (dry season). Typhoon season July–October. Surfers prefer August–December.

input: Is Catanduanes safe for solo travelers?
output: Catanduanes is generally considered safe for solo travelers. The island has a low crime rate and locals are known for being friendly and helpful. As with any rural destination, basic precautions apply: avoid remote areas at night, keep valuables secure, and always inform your accommodation of your plans for the day.
title: Catanduanes Safety for Solo Travelers
summary_offline: Catanduanes is generally safe for solo travelers. Low crime rate. Avoid remote areas at night.

input: Is there WiFi or mobile signal in Catanduanes?
output: Mobile signal is available in most populated areas of Catanduanes, particularly in Virac and municipal centers. Globe and Smart are the main providers. Signal can be weak or absent in remote barangays and mountain areas. Most accommodations in Virac offer WiFi, though speeds may be slow. Download offline maps before exploring.
title: Mobile Signal and WiFi in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Globe and Smart signal in main towns. Weak in remote areas. Virac has WiFi in most accommodations. Download offline maps.

input: What is the currency used in Catanduanes and are there ATMs?
output: The Philippine Peso (PHP) is the currency used. ATMs are available in Virac town center from BDO, Landbank, and Metrobank. Outside of Virac, ATMs are scarce. It is strongly recommended to withdraw enough cash before exploring other municipalities as most tourist spots and small establishments do not accept cards.
title: ATMs and Currency in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Philippine Peso used. ATMs (BDO, Landbank, Metrobank) in Virac only. Bring cash for other municipalities.

input: What language do people speak in Catanduanes?
output: The main language spoken in Catanduanes is Viracnon (also called Catandunganon), a regional language distinct from other Bicolano dialects. Filipino (Tagalog) is also widely understood, and English is spoken in tourist areas, hotels, and restaurants. Basic Tagalog phrases go a long way with locals.
title: Language in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Main language is Viracnon (Catandunganon). Filipino and English also spoken. Tagalog works well with locals.

input: What local food should I try in Catanduanes?
output: Catanduanes is known for several local delicacies: laing (taro leaves in coconut milk), Catanduanes pili nut products, bicol express, freshly caught seafood, pastillas (milk candy), and tinumok (ground coconut wrapped in taro leaves). The Virac Public Market is the best place to find fresh local produce and snacks.
title: Local Food to Try in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Must-try: laing, pili nut products, bicol express, fresh seafood, pastillas, tinumok. Best found at Virac Public Market.

input: What souvenirs can I buy in Catanduanes?
output: Popular souvenirs from Catanduanes include pili nut products (candy, oil, snacks), woven baskets and bags made from abaca or rattan, dried seafood, pastillas, local honey, and bottled laing. The Virac Public Market and souvenir shops near the town center are the best places to shop.
title: Souvenirs from Catanduanes
summary_offline: Best souvenirs: pili products, woven abaca goods, dried seafood, pastillas, local honey. Buy at Virac market.

input: Is Catanduanes good for snorkeling?
output: Yes, Catanduanes has several good snorkeling spots. Tuwad-Tuwadan Blue Lagoon, Cagnipa Cove, Palumbanes Island, and parts of the coastline near Pandan and Caramoran are known for clear waters and coral reefs. Bring your own gear as rentals are not always available at remote spots.
title: Snorkeling in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Best snorkeling: Tuwad-Tuwadan, Cagnipa Cove, Palumbanes Island. Bring your own gear.

input: What is the weather like in Catanduanes?
output: Catanduanes has a tropical climate with two seasons: dry (March to June) and wet (July to February). The province is one of the first in the Philippines to feel the impact of typhoons from the Pacific. Average temperatures range from 24–32°C year-round. Humidity is high, especially from June to October.
title: Catanduanes Weather
summary_offline: Tropical climate. Dry: March–June. Wet/typhoon-prone: July–February. Temperatures 24–32°C.

input: What is a typical budget for a 3-day trip to Catanduanes?
output: A budget traveler can get by on PHP 1,500–2,500 per day covering a basic guesthouse (PHP 500–800/night), meals (PHP 200–400/day), local transport (PHP 200–400/day), and entrance fees (PHP 100–200/day). A comfortable mid-range trip runs PHP 3,000–5,000 per day. Exclude airfare and ferry in these estimates.
title: Budget for a Trip to Catanduanes
summary_offline: Budget trip: PHP 1,500–2,500/day. Mid-range: PHP 3,000–5,000/day. Excludes transport to the island.

input: Are there hospitals or clinics in Catanduanes?
output: The main hospital in Catanduanes is Catanduanes Provincial Hospital in Virac. There are also rural health units (RHUs) in each municipality. For serious medical cases, patients are typically transferred to Legazpi City or Manila. It is highly recommended to bring any prescription medications as options are limited.
title: Medical Facilities in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Catanduanes Provincial Hospital in Virac. Rural health units in each municipality. Bring your own medications.

input: Is it safe to swim at Catanduanes beaches?
output: Most beaches in Catanduanes are safe for swimming during the dry season. However, some beaches like Puraran are known for strong currents and are primarily for surfing, not leisure swimming. Always check with locals about current conditions and avoid swimming alone or after dark. Jellyfish and sea urchins can be present in some areas.
title: Beach Safety in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Most beaches safe during dry season. Puraran has strong currents — surfing beach. Ask locals about conditions. Avoid swimming alone.

input: What are the top tourist attractions in Catanduanes?
output: The top tourist attractions in Catanduanes are: Puraran Beach (world-class surfing), Binurong Point (dramatic viewpoint), Tuwad-Tuwadan Blue Lagoon (crystal-clear waters), Nahulugan Falls, Maribina Falls, Luyang Cave, Cagnipa Rolling Hills, Twin Rock Beach Resort, Mamangal Beach, Museo De Catanduanes, and Palumbanes Island.
title: Top Tourist Attractions in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Top attractions: Puraran Beach, Binurong Point, Tuwad-Tuwadan, Nahulugan Falls, Maribina Falls, Luyang Cave, Cagnipa Hills, Twin Rock, Mamangal Beach, Museo De Catanduanes.

input: What is Catanduanes known for?
output: Catanduanes is nicknamed the 'Land of the Howling Winds' and is famous for its world-class surfing at Puraran Beach, stunning waterfalls, diverse coastlines, rolling hills, and unspoiled natural scenery. It is also known for its pili nuts, resilient people, and being one of the most typhoon-exposed islands in the Philippines.
title: What Catanduanes Is Known For
summary_offline: Catanduanes: 'Land of the Howling Winds.' Famous for Puraran surfing, waterfalls, pili nuts, rolling hills, and coastal scenery.

input: What are the municipalities of Catanduanes?
output: Catanduanes has 11 municipalities: Virac (the capital), Baras, Bato, Caramoran, Gigmoto, Pandan, Panganiban, San Andres, San Miguel, Viga, and Bagamanoc. Each municipality has its own distinct attractions, from coastal beaches to inland waterfalls.
title: Municipalities of Catanduanes
summary_offline: Catanduanes has 11 municipalities: Virac, Baras, Bato, Caramoran, Gigmoto, Pandan, Panganiban, San Andres, San Miguel, Viga, Bagamanoc.

input: Can I do island hopping in Catanduanes?
output: Yes, island hopping is possible in Catanduanes. Notable nearby islands include Palumbanes Island, Pusgo Island, and smaller islets off the western coast. Boat charters can be arranged through local fishermen or travel agencies in Virac. Prices range from PHP 600–2,000 per boat depending on the destination and number of stops.
title: Island Hopping in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Island hopping available to Palumbanes, Pusgo, and nearby islets. Boat charter PHP 600–2,000. Arrange through local fishermen.

input: What are good day trips from Virac?
output: Great day trips from Virac include: Maribina Falls (45 mins), Hinagasan Falls (40 mins), Mamangal Beach (50 mins), Twin Rock Beach Resort (35 mins), Museo De Catanduanes (in town), Summit View Park (30 mins), and Batalay Mangrove Eco Park (30 mins). For longer days, Nahulugan Falls (1 hr) and Puraran Beach (1.5–2 hrs) are popular.
title: Day Trips from Virac
summary_offline: Day trips from Virac: Maribina Falls, Hinagasan Falls, Mamangal Beach, Twin Rock, Museo De Catanduanes, Summit View Park. Puraran Beach for longer trips.

input: Is Catanduanes worth visiting?
output: Yes, Catanduanes is worth visiting for those seeking an off-the-beaten-path Philippine island experience. It offers world-class surfing, untouched waterfalls, rolling hills, clear lagoons, and a laid-back local culture. It receives far fewer tourists than Palawan or Siargao, making it ideal for travelers who want fewer crowds and more authenticity.
title: Is Catanduanes Worth Visiting?
summary_offline: Catanduanes is an off-the-beaten-path gem: surfing, waterfalls, hills, lagoons, fewer crowds than Palawan or Siargao.

input: What is the elevation and terrain of Catanduanes?
output: Catanduanes is a mountainous island with a rugged interior and diverse coastal terrain. The highest peak is Mount Cagmasoso at approximately 700–800 meters above sea level. The interior is covered in tropical forest, with rolling hills in the north, river valleys, and a dramatic eastern coastline facing the Pacific Ocean.
title: Catanduanes Terrain and Elevation
summary_offline: Mountainous island. Highest point Mount Cagmasoso ~700–800m. Tropical forest interior, rolling hills in north, Pacific coastline.

input: Are there convenience stores in Catanduanes?
output: There are no large chain convenience stores like 7-Eleven in Catanduanes. However, Virac has several sari-sari stores (small neighborhood shops), mini-marts, and a public market that sell basic supplies, snacks, and toiletries. Outside Virac, supplies become more limited, so stock up before heading to remote areas.
title: Convenience Stores in Catanduanes
summary_offline: No 7-Eleven in Catanduanes. Virac has sari-sari stores and mini-marts. Stock up before heading to remote areas.

input: Where is The Lumber Hotel located?
output: The Lumber Hotel is located along the main road in Virac, Catanduanes. It is one of the most prominent hotels in the capital and is easily accessible from the Virac town center, port, and airport.
title: The Lumber Hotel Location
summary_offline: The Lumber Hotel is on the main road in Virac — prominent, accessible from port and airport.

input: How much does a room cost at The Lumber Hotel?
output: The Lumber Hotel is one of the more upscale accommodations in Catanduanes. Room rates typically range from PHP 2,500–5,000 per night depending on room type and season. It offers standard, deluxe, and suite options. Rates include access to hotel facilities. Book directly or via online booking platforms — advance booking is recommended during peak season (April to June and Christmas holidays).
title: The Lumber Hotel Room Rates
summary_offline: The Lumber Hotel rates: PHP 2,500–5,000/night. Standard to suite options. Book in advance for peak season (Apr–Jun, Dec).

input: Is The Lumber Hotel the best hotel in Catanduanes?
output: The Lumber Hotel is widely considered one of the top hotel options in Virac and Catanduanes for travelers wanting full amenities. Its swimming pool, restaurant, and central location make it stand out. For beach-front experience, Twin Rock Beach Resort is also highly rated. For surfers, the resorts in Puraran are more convenient. The Lumber is best for those who prioritize comfort and facilities while based in Virac.
title: Is The Lumber Hotel the Best in Catanduanes
summary_offline: The Lumber Hotel is top-tier in Virac for amenities (pool, restaurant, central location). Twin Rock is best for beachfront; Puraran resorts for surfers.

input: How do I get to Hinik-Hinik Falls from Virac?
output: To reach Hinik-Hinik Falls from Virac, take a van or bus heading to Caramoran (approximately 1.5 to 2 hours, PHP 100–150). From Caramoran town, hire a habal-habal or ask locals for directions to the falls. A local guide is recommended as the trail is not heavily marked. Bring water, insect repellent, and sturdy footwear for the hike.
title: Hinik-Hinik Falls Directions
summary_offline: Virac to Hinik-Hinik Falls: van to Caramoran (1.5–2 hrs, PHP 100–150) then habal-habal. Get a local guide — trail is unmarked.

input: What is the entrance fee at Hinik-Hinik Falls?
output: Hinik-Hinik Falls is a barangay-managed natural site. Entrance fees are minimal — typically PHP 30–50 per person for the environmental fee. A local guide fee may also apply (PHP 100–200 per group). Exact fees can vary so confirm at the barangay hall before your visit. Bring cash as no electronic payments are accepted.
title: Hinik-Hinik Falls Entrance Fee
summary_offline: Hinik-Hinik Falls fees: ~PHP 30–50 entrance + PHP 100–200 guide fee. Cash only. Confirm at barangay hall.

input: Is Hinik-Hinik Falls worth visiting?
output: Hinik-Hinik Falls is worth visiting for travelers who enjoy off-the-beaten-path nature experiences. Because it is in Caramoran and less visited than Maribina or Nahulugan Falls, you are likely to have it nearly to yourself. The falls are set in lush forested terrain. The main downside is the longer travel time from Virac and the rougher roads to Caramoran. Best combined with a visit to Tuwad-Tuwadan Blue Lagoon which is also in Caramoran.
title: Is Hinik-Hinik Falls Worth Visiting
summary_offline: Hinik-Hinik Falls: quiet, off-the-beaten-path. Worth it if you enjoy uncrowded nature. Combine with Tuwad-Tuwadan Lagoon — both in Caramoran.

input: Where is La Prisa Campsite in Catanduanes?
output: La Prisa Campsite is a camping destination in Catanduanes offering an outdoor overnight stay experience in a natural setting. It caters to travelers who want a camping-style accommodation option beyond standard hotels. Contact the property directly for exact location, directions, and booking as its remote setting may require advance coordination.
title: La Prisa Campsite Location
summary_offline: La Prisa Campsite: camping accommodation in Catanduanes. Contact property directly for location and booking — remote setting requires advance coordination.

input: Is there camping available in Catanduanes?
output: Yes, camping is available in Catanduanes. La Prisa Campsite is one option for organized camping. Some travelers also camp near waterfall sites and clifftop areas with permission from barangay authorities. Fleur de Lilies Glamping Farm offers a glamping (tent with comfort) alternative. For beach camping, ask at the barangay level for permitted sites. Always leave no trace and be aware of weather conditions — typhoon season is not suitable for camping.
title: Camping in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Camping in Catanduanes: La Prisa Campsite (organized), Fleur de Lilies (glamping), or barangay-permitted sites near falls/beaches. Avoid typhoon season.

input: What is Hiyop Highlands?
output: Hiyop Highlands is a highland destination in Catanduanes known for its cool temperatures, scenic landscape, and views over the island. It is suitable for travelers seeking mountain and highland scenery as a contrast to the coastal attractions. The area offers trekking and photography opportunities.
title: Hiyop Highlands Overview
summary_offline: Hiyop Highlands: cool highland destination with scenic views over Catanduanes. Good for trekking and photography.

input: How do I get to Hiyop Highlands?
output: Hiyop Highlands is best reached by private vehicle or rented motorcycle from Virac. The road to the highlands involves uphill driving on rural roads. Habal-habal hire from the nearest town is also an option. Ask locals at the barangay level for the most current road conditions and directions — signage can be limited. Bring a jacket as temperatures are noticeably cooler at altitude.
title: Hiyop Highlands Directions
summary_offline: Hiyop Highlands: reach by private vehicle or habal-habal. Uphill rural roads. Ask locals for directions. Bring a jacket — noticeably cooler at altitude.

input: What is Pandan Lighthouse?
output: Pandan Lighthouse is a lighthouse located in the municipality of Pandan in northwestern Catanduanes. Like other lighthouses on the island, it serves as a navigation beacon for vessels and offers scenic views of the coastline. Pandan is one of the island's more remote municipalities, about 2–3 hours from Virac. Visiting the lighthouse is best combined with exploring the Pandan Seashore Park nearby.
title: Pandan Lighthouse Overview
summary_offline: Pandan Lighthouse is in Pandan municipality (~2–3 hrs from Virac). Scenic coastline views. Combine with Pandan Seashore Park nearby.

input: How far is Pandan from Virac?
output: Pandan municipality is approximately 70–80 km from Virac by road, making it one of the farthest municipalities from the capital. Travel time is about 2 to 3 hours by van or private vehicle due to winding mountain and coastal roads. Vans depart from the Virac terminal heading to Pandan but may have limited schedules. Check departure times at the terminal. A full-day trip is needed to visit Pandan and return.
title: Distance Virac to Pandan
summary_offline: Pandan is ~70–80 km from Virac, 2–3 hrs by road. Limited van schedules from Virac terminal. Allocate a full day for a Pandan visit and return.

input: Where is Thirstea café in Virac?
output: Thirstea is a café in Virac, Catanduanes known for its milk teas, local drinks, and café snacks. It is a popular spot with younger locals and tourists looking for a relaxed spot in town. Located in the Virac town area, it is within walking distance of the main commercial strip.
title: Thirstea Café Virac
summary_offline: Thirstea is a café in Virac serving milk teas and snacks. Popular with locals and tourists. Near Virac's main commercial area.

input: What does Thirstea serve? Is it good?
output: Thirstea in Virac is known primarily for its milk teas, fruit teas, and local refreshments. It also serves light café snacks and pastries. It is a good option for a mid-afternoon break or a sweet drink after sightseeing in Virac. The prices are affordable, making it popular with locals and budget travelers.
title: Thirstea Menu and Review
summary_offline: Thirstea serves milk teas, fruit teas, and light snacks. Affordable prices, popular for a refreshment stop in Virac.

input: Where is Kape't Tagpuan Sa Isla café?
output: Kape't Tagpuan Sa Isla is a café in Catanduanes that serves local coffee and light meals in a relaxed setting. The name translates roughly to 'Coffee Meetup on the Island,' reflecting its casual, community-oriented atmosphere. It is located in the Virac area and is a good spot for a local coffee experience.
title: Kape't Tagpuan Sa Isla Café
summary_offline: Kape't Tagpuan Sa Isla: local coffee shop in Virac with casual atmosphere. Good for a local coffee experience and light meals.

input: What cafes are there in Virac Catanduanes?
output: Virac has a growing café scene. Notable cafes include: Liz & Bel Café (local favorites, good coffee), Thirstea (milk teas and refreshments), Kape't Tagpuan Sa Isla (local coffee, relaxed setting), ARDCI Café (inside ARDCI area, good for a quick coffee), Blue Café, Mayan Café, and Teaholic. Most are concentrated along or near the main road in Virac. Coffee culture has grown significantly — expect good espresso-based drinks at most.
title: Cafes in Virac Catanduanes
summary_offline: Virac cafes: Liz & Bel Café, Thirstea, Kape't Tagpuan, ARDCI Café, Blue Café, Mayan Café, Teaholic. Most near the main road.

input: Where is Sea Breeze 2001 restaurant in Virac?
output: Sea Breeze 2001 is one of the most established local restaurants in Virac, popular with local families, government workers, and regular visitors. It serves Filipino cuisine at honest prices and has been a Virac dining institution for years. Located in Virac proper, it is a reliable choice for a sit-down Filipino meal.
title: Sea Breeze 2001 Restaurant Virac
summary_offline: Sea Breeze 2001: established Virac restaurant serving Filipino food at local prices. Popular with families and workers. Reliable sit-down dining.

input: Where is Buddy's Mami House in Virac?
output: Buddy's Mami House is a casual eatery in Virac known for its noodle soup dishes (mami) and affordable local meals. It's a go-to for locals wanting a quick, inexpensive hot meal. Located in the Virac town area, it's particularly popular for breakfast and lunch.
title: Buddy's Mami House Virac
summary_offline: Buddy's Mami House: casual Virac eatery known for noodle soup and affordable local meals. Popular for breakfast and lunch.

input: Where is Solong Falls in Catanduanes?
output: Solong Falls is a waterfall located in Catanduanes. It is one of the less-visited falls on the island, making it a good option for travelers seeking a quieter nature experience. Getting there requires local transport and a short hike. Ask at the nearest barangay for directions and guide availability.
title: Solong Falls Location
summary_offline: Solong Falls: less-visited waterfall in Catanduanes. Requires local transport and short hike. Ask barangay for directions.

input: Where is Paday Falls in Catanduanes?
output: Paday Falls is a waterfall destination in Catanduanes offering a natural swimming area and lush forested surroundings. It is accessed via local transport and a short trail. Entrance fees are minimal. Paday Falls is a good secondary waterfall visit when exploring the southern part of the island around Bato.
title: Paday Falls Location
summary_offline: Paday Falls: natural waterfall with swimming area. Accessed by local transport + short trail. Good secondary visit when exploring southern Catanduanes.

input: Solo travel tips for Catanduanes
output: Tips for solo travel in Catanduanes: rent a motorcycle if you are comfortable riding (most flexible option), or hire a tricycle or habal-habal for shorter trips. Join group van tours organized by accommodations for major sights like Binurong Point — this saves cost and lets you meet other travelers. Book accommodation in advance for peak months. Download offline maps (Google Maps works offline). Bring enough cash from Virac before heading out. Learn basic Tagalog phrases — it goes a long way in rural areas.
title: Solo Travel Tips Catanduanes
summary_offline: Solo tips: rent motorcycle for flexibility, or habal-habal for short trips. Join group tours at Binurong Point. Download offline maps. Bring cash from Virac. Learn basic Tagalog.

input: Is it easy to travel around Catanduanes alone?
output: Getting around Catanduanes solo is manageable but requires planning. Vans and buses connect Virac to main municipalities on somewhat regular schedules. Habal-habal (motorcycle taxis) are available in most towns. Renting a motorcycle is the most flexible option for solo travel. For remote sites like Binurong Point, hiring a driver-guide for the day is easiest. Public transport runs during daytime — do not count on late evening transport outside Virac.
title: Getting Around Catanduanes Solo
summary_offline: Solo transport: vans/buses for main towns, habal-habal for short trips, motorcycle rental for flexibility. Remote sites need a hired driver. No late evening transport outside Virac.

input: Can a solo female traveler visit Catanduanes?
output: Yes, Catanduanes is a welcoming destination for solo female travelers. The island's rural, community-oriented culture means locals are generally respectful and helpful. Virac is safe to walk during the day. Avoid isolated areas alone after dark. Use established accommodations rather than informal stays. When hiking to remote falls or viewpoints, always hire a local guide — both for safety and navigation. Several solo female travel bloggers have written positively about visiting Catanduanes.
title: Solo Female Travel in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Safe for solo female travelers. Respectful rural community, helpful locals. Hire guides for remote hikes. Avoid isolated areas after dark. Use established accommodations.

input: Is Catanduanes good for couples or a honeymoon?
output: Catanduanes is an excellent destination for couples and honeymooners who enjoy nature. The island's secluded beaches, dramatic viewpoints like Binurong Point at sunrise, and peaceful waterfalls create very romantic settings. Twin Rock Beach Resort and Fleur de Lilies Glamping Farm are particularly popular for couples. The island's unhurried pace, scenic roads, and absence of crowds add to the intimacy. Book a beachfront cottage at Twin Rock or a glamping tent for a memorable experience.
title: Catanduanes for Couples and Honeymoon
summary_offline: Excellent for couples: Binurong Point sunrise, Twin Rock beachfront, Fleur de Lilies glamping. Peaceful, uncrowded, and scenic — great honeymoon destination.

input: What are the most romantic spots in Catanduanes?
output: The most romantic spots in Catanduanes: Binurong Point at sunrise (dramatic cliffs and sea views best enjoyed at first light), Twin Rock Beach at sunset with the twin rock formations in the water, Tuwad-Tuwadan Blue Lagoon for a private swimming experience, any of the quieter waterfalls like Macutal Falls, and a sunset dinner at a restaurant along the Virac coastline. The island's natural beauty and seclusion make almost every scenic spot feel intimate.
title: Romantic Spots in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Romantic spots: Binurong Point sunrise, Twin Rock sunset, Tuwad-Tuwadan Lagoon, Macutal Falls, Virac coastline dinner. Island seclusion makes everything feel intimate.

input: How much money should I bring to Catanduanes for 3 days?
output: For a 3-day budget trip: accommodation PHP 600–1,500/night (budget inn), transport PHP 500–1,000 total (vans + habal-habal), food PHP 300–600/day (local eateries), entrance fees PHP 200–500 total, miscellaneous PHP 500. Total budget: approximately PHP 4,000–7,000 for 3 days. For a mid-range trip with hotel, van hire, and restaurant meals, budget PHP 10,000–15,000. Always bring extra cash — ATMs are only in Virac and can run out.
title: Budget for 3 Days in Catanduanes
summary_offline: 3-day budget: PHP 4,000–7,000 (backpacker) or PHP 10,000–15,000 (mid-range). Bring extra cash — ATMs only in Virac and can run out.

input: What is the cheapest way to travel in Catanduanes?
output: The cheapest way to travel in Catanduanes: stay at budget inns in Virac (PHP 600–1,200/night), use public vans and buses between towns (PHP 30–150 per trip), hire habal-habal for local trips (PHP 30–100), eat at carinderias and the public market (PHP 80–150 per meal), and bring your own water bottle. Coming by ferry from Tabaco is much cheaper than flying. The main cost-saver is avoiding private van hire — using public transport takes longer but cuts transport costs significantly.
title: Cheapest Way to Travel Catanduanes
summary_offline: Cheapest travel: budget inns (PHP 600–1,200), public vans (PHP 30–150), habal-habal (PHP 30–100), carinderias (PHP 80–150/meal). Ferry over flying. Avoid private van hire.

input: Are there entrance fees at the waterfalls in Catanduanes?
output: Most waterfalls in Catanduanes charge a small entrance or environmental fee managed by the local barangay. Typical fees: Maribina Falls PHP 20–50 per person, Nahulugan Falls PHP 30–50, Macutal Falls PHP 30–50, Hinik-Hinik Falls PHP 30–50, Hinagasan Falls PHP 20–30. Some also charge a guide fee (PHP 100–200 per group). Exact fees can change — confirm on arrival. Always bring cash in small bills.
title: Waterfall Entrance Fees in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Waterfall fees: Maribina PHP 20–50, Nahulugan PHP 30–50, Macutal PHP 30–50, Hinagasan PHP 20–30. Plus guide fee PHP 100–200/group. Bring small bills.

input: Where is the van terminal in Virac?
output: The main van and bus terminal in Virac is located near the town center, within walking distance of the port area. Vans to various municipalities (Bato, Baras, Caramoran, Pandan, etc.) depart from this terminal throughout the day, with most routes running from early morning until early afternoon. For early-morning departures to distant municipalities, arrive at the terminal by 6–7 AM to secure a seat.
title: Van Terminal in Virac
summary_offline: Virac van/bus terminal is near the town center and port. Vans to all municipalities. Most routes run 6 AM–early afternoon. Arrive early for distant towns.

input: How much does a tricycle cost in Virac?
output: Tricycles in Virac charge PHP 10–20 per person for short routes within the town center. For longer in-town trips or if you hire the whole tricycle (special trip), expect PHP 50–150 depending on distance. Tricycles are the main short-distance transport within Virac town. They do not usually travel to tourist spots outside town — for those, use habal-habal or hire a van.
title: Tricycle Fares in Virac
summary_offline: Tricycle in Virac: PHP 10–20/person for short routes, PHP 50–150 for special trips. Town-only transport — use habal-habal or van for tourist sites.

input: How much does it cost to rent a motorcycle in Catanduanes?
output: Motorcycle rentals in Catanduanes typically cost PHP 500–800 per day for a standard 110–125cc motorcycle. Some accommodations and rental shops offer them informally — ask your hotel or inn. An international or Philippine driver's license is technically required. Check the motorcycle condition carefully before renting — tire quality and brakes matter on Catanduanes' winding mountain roads. Fuel up in Virac before heading to remote areas as petrol stations are scarce outside the capital.
title: Motorcycle Rental in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Motorcycle rental: PHP 500–800/day. Ask at your inn. Check tires and brakes carefully. Fill up fuel in Virac — petrol stations scarce outside capital.

input: Can I hire a van with driver in Catanduanes? How much?
output: Yes, hiring a private van with driver is common and recommended for day trips to multiple sites. Rates typically range from PHP 2,500–4,500 for a full day depending on destinations, distance, and negotiation. Most accommodations in Virac can arrange van hire or recommend trusted drivers. A van can usually accommodate 6–8 people, making it cost-effective for groups. For Binurong Point, Puraran, and waterfall routes, a whole-day van hire is the most convenient option.
title: Van Hire in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Van hire with driver: PHP 2,500–4,500/day. Fits 6–8 people. Ask your hotel to arrange. Best option for Binurong Point, Puraran, and multi-stop day trips.

input: When is the best time to visit Catanduanes?
output: The best time to visit Catanduanes is from March to June (dry season). April and May offer the most reliable sunny weather, calm seas, and the best conditions for beach activities, waterfall hikes, and cliff viewpoints. The typhoon season runs from August to November — seas are rough, some roads may be impassable after storms, and flights/ferries face cancellations. December to February brings cooler, sometimes rainy weather but is still manageable. March to June is the safest bet.
title: Best Time to Visit Catanduanes
summary_offline: Best time: March–June (dry, sunny, calm seas). Peak is April–May. Avoid Aug–Nov typhoon season. Dec–Feb is cooler and wetter but manageable.

input: What is the weather in Catanduanes in December?
output: December in Catanduanes is cooler and can be rainy, but the typhoon season is winding down. Expect occasional showers but generally more settled weather compared to September–November. Seas are calmer in December, making ferry travel more reliable. It is a reasonable time to visit with thinner crowds. Pack a light rain jacket and layers for cooler evenings.
title: Catanduanes Weather in December
summary_offline: December: cooler, occasional showers, typhoon season winding down. Calmer seas than Oct–Nov. Light crowds. Bring a rain jacket.

input: What is the weather in Catanduanes in April?
output: April is one of the best months to visit Catanduanes. The weather is dry, hot, and sunny with minimal rainfall. Seas are calm, making it ideal for swimming, surfing, island hopping, and beach activities. Most waterfalls are accessible and the trails are dry. It is also peak tourist season so accommodation books fast — reserve well in advance.
title: Catanduanes Weather in April
summary_offline: April: best month — dry, sunny, calm seas. Ideal for beaches, surfing, island hopping, and trails. Peak tourist season — book accommodation in advance.

input: Is there a hospital in Catanduanes?
output: Yes. The main public hospital is Catanduanes Provincial Hospital, located in Virac. It handles most medical emergencies on the island. There are also rural health units (RHUs) in other municipalities for basic healthcare. For serious cases requiring specialist care, patients are transferred to Legazpi City in Albay via ferry or air. Always bring a personal first aid kit and any prescription medications as some drugs may not be available locally.
title: Hospital in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Main hospital: Catanduanes Provincial Hospital in Virac. RHUs in each municipality for basic care. Serious cases airlifted/ferried to Legazpi. Bring your own meds and first aid.

input: Are there pharmacies in Catanduanes?
output: Yes, pharmacies are available in Virac town center, including Mercury Drug and other local pharmacies along the main road. Common medications, wound care, and basic medical supplies are available. In smaller municipalities, only small sari-sari stores may stock limited over-the-counter medicines. Bring enough supply of any prescription medication for your entire trip as specialty drugs may not be available on the island.
title: Pharmacies in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Pharmacies in Virac: Mercury Drug and local pharmacies on main road. Limited meds in smaller towns. Bring all prescription meds from Manila — specialty drugs unavailable locally.

input: When is the best time for surfing in Catanduanes?
output: The best surfing season in Catanduanes is from September to December when swells from Pacific typhoons generate the most powerful and consistent waves at Puraran Beach. October and November are peak surf months with waves regularly reaching 6–10 feet. For beginners and intermediate surfers, the shoulder months of July–August and January–February also offer good rideable waves. April to June is the flattest period — not ideal for surfing but perfect for beach relaxation.
title: Best Time for Surfing in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Best surf: Sep–Dec (peak Oct–Nov, waves 6–10 ft). Shoulder: Jul–Aug and Jan–Feb. Apr–Jun is flattest. Puraran Beach is the primary surf site.

input: Are there surf lessons in Catanduanes?
output: Yes, surf lessons are available at Puraran Beach in Baras. Most surf resorts there — including Pacific Surfers Paradise Resort, Jo Surf Inn, and Titaay Surfers Inn — offer beginner and intermediate surf lessons with local instructors. Lesson rates typically range from PHP 500–1,000 per session including board rental. Instructors are experienced with the local breaks. The best time for lessons is early morning (6–10 AM) when winds are lighter.
title: Surf Lessons in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Surf lessons at Puraran Beach: Pacific Surfers, Jo Surf Inn, Titaay Surfers Inn. PHP 500–1,000/session with board. Best lessons in the morning (6–10 AM).

input: What is Jo Surf Inn in Puraran?
output: Jo Surf Inn is one of the surf camp accommodations located at Puraran Beach in Baras, Catanduanes. It caters primarily to surfers and offers budget-friendly rooms or dormitory-style accommodations close to the waves. The inn provides surf board rentals and can arrange surf lessons with local instructors. It is part of the cluster of small surf resorts that make up the Puraran surf community.
title: Jo Surf Inn Puraran
summary_offline: Jo Surf Inn: budget surf camp at Puraran Beach. Board rentals and surf lessons. Part of Puraran's surf resort cluster.

input: What is Titaay Surfers Inn?
output: Titaay Surfers Inn is a surf accommodation located at Puraran Beach in Baras, Catanduanes. It offers rooms and basic facilities for surfers staying at the Puraran break. Surfboard rentals and access to the beach are the main draws. It is part of the small community of surf-focused lodging options at Puraran, suited for budget-conscious surfers.
title: Titaay Surfers Inn
summary_offline: Titaay Surfers Inn: budget surf lodging at Puraran Beach. Surfboard rentals, beach access. Good for budget surfers in the Puraran community.

input: Anong pinaka-magandang beach sa Catanduanes?
output: Ang pinaka-popular na beach sa Catanduanes para sa surfing ay ang Puraran Beach sa Baras — ito ay world-class surfing destination. Para sa pamilya at swimming, ang Twin Rock Beach Resort sa Virac ang pinakaligtas at pinakamaginhawa. Para sa mas tahimik at hindi masyadong sikat, ang Mamangal Beach, Campilan Beach, at Toytoy Beach ay magagandang pagpipilian.
title: Pinaka-magandang Beach sa Catanduanes
summary_offline: Best beaches: Puraran (surfing), Twin Rock (family swimming), Mamangal/Campilan/Toytoy (quieter options).

input: Magkano ang pasahe papunta Catanduanes?
output: Ang pasahe papunta Catanduanes ay depende sa paraan ng biyahe. Via eroplano: ang Cebu Pacific at SkyJet ay may flights mula NAIA papunta Virac Airport (VIC), karaniwang PHP 1,500–4,000 one-way depende sa book-ahead. Via ferry: bus o van papunta Tabaco City, Albay (PHP 300–500 mula Manila via Philtranco/DLTB), tapos RoRo ferry Tabaco-Virac (PHP 200–350 per person). Ang ferry ay mas mura pero mas matagal ang biyahe.
title: Pasahe Papunta Catanduanes
summary_offline: Pasahe: eroplano (PHP 1,500–4,000 one-way via Cebu Pacific/SkyJet) o bus to Tabaco (PHP 300–500) + ferry (PHP 200–350). Ferry mas mura, mas matagal.

input: Saan pwedeng manggaling ng cash sa Catanduanes?
output: May mga ATM sa Virac na mula sa BDO, Landbank, at Metrobank. Ito ang pinaka-maaasahang paraan para mag-withdraw ng pera. Sa ibang munisipyo (Bato, Baras, Caramoran, etc.) ay wala pang ATM kaya kailangan mag-withdraw muna sa Virac bago lumayo. Sa peak season at pagkatapos ng holidays, minsan ang ATM ay walang pera — mas mabuting mag-withdraw ng malaki bago umalis ng Virac.
title: Saan Mag-withdraw ng Pera sa Catanduanes
summary_offline: ATMs sa Virac: BDO, Landbank, Metrobank. Walang ATM sa ibang munisipyo. Mag-withdraw ng sapat bago lumayo — minsan walang pera sa ATM sa peak season.

input: Pwede bang lumusong sa mga talon sa Catanduanes?
output: Oo, maraming mga talon sa Catanduanes na pwedeng lumusong o lumangoy. Ang Maribina Falls ay may mababaw at malalim na pool na angkop para sa lahat ng edad. Ang Nahulugan Falls ay may mas malalim na pool. Ang Macutal Falls ay may magandang swimming area. Mahalaga na sumunod sa mga alituntunin ng barangay at huwag lumangoy kung may malakas na ulan o baha.
title: Swimming sa mga Talon ng Catanduanes
summary_offline: Malusong na talon: Maribina Falls (mababaw at malalim na pool), Nahulugan Falls, Macutal Falls. Huwag lumusong kung may ulan o baha.

input: Are there bars in Catanduanes?
output: Catanduanes has a modest but enjoyable nightlife scene, mostly centered in Virac. Notable spots: E-Lounge Resto Bar (popular for drinks and light food), Black Alley (bar with drinks), ARDCI Skydeck Lounge (elevated bar with a view), and Tarahid KTV Bar for karaoke. Most bars operate until 10 PM–midnight. It is not a party island — the nightlife is low-key and local.
title: Bars and Nightlife in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Virac bars: E-Lounge Resto Bar, ARDCI Skydeck Lounge, Tarahid KTV. Low-key nightlife, closes by 10 PM–midnight. Not a party island.

input: Where can I watch the sunset in Catanduanes?
output: Best sunset spots in Catanduanes: Cagnipa Rolling Hills for open sky panoramas, Twin Rock Beach Resort (the twin rocks silhouette beautifully at sunset), Mamangal Beach for a western-facing beach sunset, ARDCI Skydeck Lounge in Virac for an elevated view over the town and coast, and the Virac Port area for harbor sunset views. Binurong Point is technically east-facing and better for sunrise, not sunset.
title: Best Sunset Spots in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Best sunsets: Cagnipa Rolling Hills, Twin Rock Beach, Mamangal Beach, ARDCI Skydeck Lounge, Virac Port. Binurong Point is a sunrise spot, not sunset.

I specialize in Catanduanes — ask me anything about the island.

input: Give me recommendations for Catanduanes
output: Great picks for Catanduanes: Binurong Point for some of the most dramatic coastal views in the Philippines, Puraran Beach for world-class surfing, Tuwad-Tuwadan Blue Lagoon for stunning turquoise waters, Maribina Falls for the most accessible waterfall swim, Luyang Cave for a unique cave exploration, and Cagnipa Rolling Hills for sweeping grassland panoramas. For food, try the local carinderia breakfast at Virac Public Market and fresh seafood in the evening. Let me know your interests and I can be more specific.
