summary_offline: Top picks: Binurong Point, Puraran Beach, Tuwad-Tuwadan Lagoon, Maribina Falls, Luyang Cave, Cagnipa Hills. Food: market carinderia breakfast + fresh seafood dinner.

input: How do I get to Bote Lighthouse from Virac?
output: Bote Lighthouse is located in Bato, about 45 minutes to 1 hour from Virac by van or bus heading south. Alight at Bato town proper then take a habal-habal (PHP 50–80) to the lighthouse trailhead. The hike to the top takes 20–30 minutes through forested terrain. No official shuttle — arrange a habal-habal driver to wait or return at a set time.
title: Bote Lighthouse Directions
summary_offline: Bote Lighthouse is in Bato, ~1 hr from Virac by van/bus then habal-habal (PHP 50–80). Hike takes 20–30 mins to the top.

input: What can I do at Bote Lighthouse?
output: Bote Lighthouse offers one of the best panoramic views of the eastern coast of Catanduanes. Activities include the short hike up, photography of the coastline and lighthouse structure, and birdwatching. The view at sunrise is particularly striking. There is no swimming at this site — it's a cliff-top viewpoint. Bring water as there are no vendors.
title: Bote Lighthouse Activities
summary_offline: Bote Lighthouse: panoramic coastal views, photography, short hike. Best at sunrise. No swimming, no vendors — bring water.

input: What time does Bote Lighthouse open? Are there fees?
output: Bote Lighthouse is accessible during daylight hours (roughly 6 AM to 5 PM). There is no formal entrance fee for the lighthouse area, but a small donation or guide tip is appreciated by locals who may assist. The lighthouse structure itself is maintained by the Philippine Coast Guard and entry inside may be restricted.
title: Bote Lighthouse Hours and Fees
summary_offline: Bote Lighthouse open daylight hours (6 AM–5 PM). No formal entrance fee but bring tip for local guides. Interior access may be restricted.

input: How do I get to Balacay Point from Virac?
output: Balacay Point is in Baras, about 1 hour from Virac. Take a van or bus heading to Baras (PHP 50–80), then hire a habal-habal from Baras town to the trailhead (PHP 80–120). The trail is steep in some parts — sturdy footwear is recommended. The site has limited signage so ask locals or arrange a guide at the barangay.
title: Balacay Point Directions
summary_offline: Balacay Point is in Baras (~1 hr from Virac). Van to Baras then habal-habal (PHP 80–120) to trailhead. Trail is steep — wear sturdy shoes.

input: What is Balacay Point like? What can I see there?
output: Balacay Point is a dramatic cliffside viewpoint in Baras offering views of the open Pacific Ocean and rugged coastline. It's less visited than Binurong Point, making it ideal for those wanting a quieter experience. The rocky cliffs are striking and there are small coves visible below. Sunrise visits are recommended. The trail passes through rural community areas — be respectful of local property.
title: Balacay Point Description
summary_offline: Balacay Point: dramatic Pacific cliffs in Baras, less crowded than Binurong Point. Great for sunrise, photography, and quiet coastal views.

input: Best time to visit Balacay Point?
output: The best time to visit Balacay Point is during the dry season (March to June) when trails are less slippery and visibility is clearest. Early morning (6–9 AM) gives the best light for photography and avoids midday heat. Avoid visiting during or after heavy rain as the trail becomes muddy and unsafe. The typhoon months (August–October) are not recommended.
title: Balacay Point Best Time to Visit
summary_offline: Visit Balacay Point March–June, mornings 6–9 AM. Avoid rainy season — trail gets slippery. Not recommended during typhoon months.

input: What is Fleur de Lilies Glamping Farm like?
output: Fleur de Lilies Glamping Farm is a unique glamping destination in Catanduanes offering tent-style accommodation in a farm setting. It's ideal for travelers who want a nature-immersed stay without roughing it completely. The property has garden areas, farm animals, and a relaxed rural atmosphere. It appeals to couples and small groups looking for something different from standard hotel accommodation.
title: Fleur de Lilies Glamping Farm Overview
summary_offline: Fleur de Lilies Glamping Farm: tent-style stays in a farm setting. Good for couples and small groups wanting nature without roughing it.

input: How much does it cost to stay at Fleur de Lilies Glamping Farm?
output: Fleur de Lilies Glamping Farm rates are in the mid-range category. Expect to pay approximately PHP 1,500–3,000 per night depending on the tent type and season. It's advisable to contact them directly to confirm current rates and availability. Booking in advance is recommended especially on weekends and holidays.
title: Fleur de Lilies Rates
summary_offline: Fleur de Lilies Glamping Farm rates: approx PHP 1,500–3,000/night. Book in advance for weekends. Confirm rates directly.

input: What activities are available at Fleur de Lilies Glamping Farm?
output: Activities at Fleur de Lilies Glamping Farm include farm exploration, star-gazing at night away from city lights, and relaxing in the natural surroundings. Some guests use it as a base for day trips to Hinagasan Falls and other Virac attractions. The farm setting gives opportunities for photography and peaceful walks. It is not a beach property.
title: Fleur de Lilies Activities
summary_offline: Fleur de Lilies: farm walks, star-gazing, photography, day trip base for Virac attractions. Not a beach property.

input: What are the amenities at Nitto Lodge in Virac?
output: Nitto Lodge is a budget accommodation in Virac with basic room amenities including air-conditioning and private bathroom. It is within walking distance of the Virac town center, making it convenient for accessing restaurants, the public market, and transport terminals. It does not have a swimming pool or restaurant on-site, but many dining options are nearby.
title: Nitto Lodge Amenities
summary_offline: Nitto Lodge: budget inn in Virac with AC and private bath. No pool or restaurant. Walking distance to market and dining.

input: How much is a room at Nitto Lodge?
output: Nitto Lodge is one of the more affordable options in Virac, with room rates typically ranging from PHP 600–1,200 per night for standard rooms. It is a good option for solo travelers or budget-conscious pairs who prioritize location over amenities.
title: Nitto Lodge Room Rates
summary_offline: Nitto Lodge rooms: approx PHP 600–1,200/night. Good budget option in Virac for solo travelers.

input: Where exactly is ARDCI Corporate Inn located?
output: ARDCI Corporate Inn is located in Virac, the capital of Catanduanes. It is situated near the town center and within reasonable distance of the Virac port and government offices. The inn caters primarily to business travelers and government guests but accepts tourists.
title: ARDCI Corporate Inn Location
summary_offline: ARDCI Corporate Inn is in Virac near the town center, port, and government offices. Caters to business travelers but accepts tourists.

input: What amenities does ARDCI Corporate Inn offer?
output: ARDCI Corporate Inn offers air-conditioned rooms with private bathrooms, and some units have amenities suited for business travelers like work desks. The inn has a functional, no-frills setup. Confirm breakfast inclusion and WiFi availability when booking as these may vary by room type.
title: ARDCI Corporate Inn Amenities
summary_offline: ARDCI Corporate Inn: AC rooms, private bath, work desks. Functional mid-budget option. Confirm WiFi and breakfast when booking.

input: What are the rates at Catanduanes Midtown Inn?
output: Catanduanes Midtown Inn offers mid-range accommodation in Virac with room rates typically between PHP 1,200–2,500 per night depending on room type. Standard rooms include air-conditioning and private bathrooms. The inn is centrally located, making it easy to access Virac's restaurants and transport options.
title: Catanduanes Midtown Inn Rates
summary_offline: Catanduanes Midtown Inn rates: PHP 1,200–2,500/night. Mid-range, AC rooms, central Virac location.

input: What amenities does Catanduanes Midtown Inn have?
output: Catanduanes Midtown Inn features air-conditioned rooms with private bathrooms and basic hotel amenities. Its central location in Virac is a key advantage. Confirm WiFi availability and parking with the property before arrival.
title: Catanduanes Midtown Inn Amenities
summary_offline: Catanduanes Midtown Inn: AC, private bath, central Virac location near market and transport. Confirm WiFi and parking on booking.

input: 2 day itinerary in Catanduanes
output: Day 1: Arrive at Virac, check in, then spend the afternoon exploring Virac town — Museo De Catanduanes, Virac Public Market, and dinner at a local restaurant. Day 2: Early start (5 AM) to Binurong Point for sunrise views, then drive south to Puraran Beach for a swim or surf lesson, lunch in Baras, stop at Maribina Falls in the afternoon, return to Virac for evening dinner.
title: 2-Day Catanduanes Itinerary
summary_offline: 2 days: Day 1 Virac orientation (Museo, market). Day 2: Binurong Point sunrise → Puraran Beach → Maribina Falls.

input: 3 day itinerary in Catanduanes
output: Day 1: Arrive in Virac, rest and explore town (market, Museo, dinner). Day 2: North route — Binurong Point sunrise, Puraran Beach surfing or swimming, lunch in Baras, return via Luyang Cave. Day 3: South route — Maribina Falls morning, Nahulugan Falls, Bato Church, Bote Lighthouse, back to Virac.
title: 3-Day Catanduanes Itinerary
summary_offline: 3 days: Day 1 Virac. Day 2 North (Binurong Point, Puraran, Luyang Cave). Day 3 South (Maribina Falls, Nahulugan, Bato Church).

input: What is a good itinerary for surfers visiting Catanduanes?
output: Base yourself in Puraran Beach in Baras — most surf resorts offer 3–7 night packages. Spend mornings surfing (best waves 6–11 AM), afternoons exploring Binurong Point, or relaxing on the beach. Day trip to Nahulugan Falls for a break from the water. Peak surf season September to December.
title: Surfer Itinerary Catanduanes
summary_offline: Surfer itinerary: base at Puraran, surf mornings, Binurong Point afternoons. Day trips to Nahulugan Falls and Luyang Cave. Peak surf Sep–Dec.

input: Nature and hiking itinerary for Catanduanes
output: Day 1: Maribina Falls, Nahulugan Falls, Bato Church. Day 2: Binurong Point hike at sunrise, Luyang Cave. Day 3: Cagnipa Rolling Hills, Tuwad-Tuwadan Blue Lagoon. Day 4 optional: Bote Lighthouse, Balacay Point, Palumbanes Island by boat.
title: Nature Itinerary Catanduanes
summary_offline: Nature itinerary: Day 1 South falls + Bato Church. Day 2 Binurong Point + Luyang Cave. Day 3 Cagnipa Hills + Tuwad Lagoon. Day 4 Bote/Balacay/Palumbanes.

input: Weekend itinerary in Catanduanes, 2 nights 3 days
output: Arrive Friday evening, check in to a Virac hotel and have dinner downtown. Saturday: rent a motorcycle or hire a van, morning at Binurong Point, Puraran Beach for lunch and swim, evening back in Virac. Sunday: Maribina Falls morning, quick stop at Nahulugan Falls, lunch in Bato, depart afternoon.
title: Weekend Trip to Catanduanes
summary_offline: Weekend (Fri–Sun): Fri check-in Virac. Sat Binurong Point + Puraran Beach. Sun Maribina + Nahulugan Falls, depart afternoon.

input: Is 4 days enough for Catanduanes?
output: Yes, 4 days is comfortable. Day 1 Virac arrival and orientation, Day 2 north route (Binurong Point, Puraran Beach, Luyang Cave), Day 3 south route (Maribina Falls, Nahulugan Falls, Bato Church, Bote Lighthouse), Day 4 morning buffer and departure. For surfers, 5–7 days is ideal.
title: Is 4 Days Enough Catanduanes
summary_offline: 4 days comfortably covers: Virac, north route (Binurong, Puraran, Cave), south route (Falls, Bato). For surfers, 5–7 days is better.

input: Is Catanduanes good for families with young children?
output: Catanduanes is generally family-friendly. Maribina Falls in Bato has calm shallow pools. Twin Rock Beach Resort has a protected beach cove safe for kids. Road conditions in rural areas can be rough — bring padding for habal-habal rides with small children.
title: Family Travel with Kids in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Family-friendly: Maribina Falls (calm pools), Twin Rock (safe cove), Virac Market. Rural roads are rough — prepare for bumpy rides with kids.

input: What are the safest beaches in Catanduanes for kids?
output: Twin Rock Beach Resort in Virac has the calmest, most sheltered beach on the island. Mamangal Beach in Virac is also relatively calm. Puraran Beach has strong surf and rip currents — not suitable for unsupervised young children.
title: Safe Beaches for Kids in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Safest for kids: Twin Rock Beach Resort (sheltered cove). Mamangal Beach also calm. Avoid Puraran for young kids — strong surf and rip currents.

input: Is there a playground or indoor activity for kids in Catanduanes?
output: Indoor or structured playground facilities are very limited in Catanduanes. For rainy days, there are no malls — a rest day at the hotel pool or a visit to Museo De Catanduanes works well. Pack games and entertainment for downtime.
title: Indoor Activities for Kids Catanduanes
summary_offline: No malls or playgrounds in Catanduanes. Kids enjoy Luyang Cave, market visit, Twin Rock swimming. Pack entertainment for rainy days.

input: Can I bring a baby or toddler to Catanduanes?
output: Yes, but plan carefully. There are no specialty baby stores — bring all diapers, formula, and supplies from Manila. Roads to remote sites can be very rough. Stick to Virac and Twin Rock Beach Resort. Avoid remote waterfalls and cliffside hikes with toddlers. Provincial hospital is in Virac only.
title: Traveling with a Baby in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Bring all baby supplies from Manila — none available locally. Stick to Virac and Twin Rock. Avoid rough trails. Provincial hospital is in Virac only.

input: What accommodation in Catanduanes is most family-friendly?
output: Twin Rock Beach Resort in Virac is the most recommended family accommodation — safe calm beach, swimming pool, and family room options. E-Crown Hotel in Virac also caters well to families with pool access.
title: Family-Friendly Accommodation Catanduanes
summary_offline: Best family accommodation: Twin Rock Beach Resort (calm beach, pool, family rooms), E-Crown Hotel (pool). Both in Virac.

input: Where can I snorkel in Catanduanes?
output: Best snorkeling: Palumbanes Island off the west coast (boat from Bato), LouCa Beach Resort in San Andres, Campilan Beach in Caramoran. Avoid Puraran Beach for snorkeling — the surf makes it unsuitable. Gear rental is limited — bring your own if possible.
title: Snorkeling Spots in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Best snorkeling: Palumbanes Island (boat from Bato), LouCa Beach Resort (San Andres), Campilan Beach (Caramoran). Bring your own gear — rentals are scarce.

input: Can I go kayaking in Catanduanes?
output: Kayaking is possible at Tuwad-Tuwadan Blue Lagoon in Caramoran — calm water ideal for casual paddlers. Some beach resorts offer kayak rentals. Sea kayaking should only be done with local guides. No dedicated kayaking outfitter — confirm availability before your trip.
title: Kayaking in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Kayaking: best at Tuwad-Tuwadan Blue Lagoon (calm water). Some resorts have rentals. Sea kayaking needs local guide. Confirm ahead.

input: Best photography spots in Catanduanes
output: Top photography spots: Binurong Point (sunrise cliffs), Puraran Beach (wave action), Cagnipa Rolling Hills (grassland panoramas), Maribina Falls (lush waterfall), Tuwad-Tuwadan Blue Lagoon (turquoise reflections), Virac Public Market (morning color and activity).
title: Photography Spots Catanduanes
summary_offline: Top photo spots: Binurong Point (sunrise cliffs), Puraran Beach (waves), Cagnipa Hills (grasslands), Maribina Falls, Tuwad Lagoon (turquoise water), Virac Market.

input: Is island hopping available in Catanduanes?
output: Island hopping is available near Bato. Palumbanes Island is the main destination, accessible by boat from the Bato coast (PHP 600–1,200). Snorkeling and white sand beach. Best months are March to June. Rough seas June–November.
title: Island Hopping in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Island hopping: mainly Palumbanes Island from Bato (PHP 600–1,200 boat). Snorkeling, white sand beach. Best March–June.

input: What outdoor activities can I do in Catanduanes?
output: Catanduanes offers: surfing (Puraran), waterfall hiking (Maribina, Nahulugan, Macutal, Hinagasan), cliff hiking (Binurong Point, Balacay Point), cave exploration (Luyang Cave), snorkeling (Palumbanes Island, Campilan), kayaking (Tuwad-Tuwadan), island hopping (Bato), grassland trekking (Cagnipa Hills), and photography throughout.
title: Outdoor Activities in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Activities: surfing, waterfall hikes, cliff hikes, cave exploration, snorkeling, kayaking, island hopping, grassland trekking.

input: Where can I buy pasalubong in Catanduanes?
output: Best pasalubong spots: Virac Public Market and pasalubong shops on the main road in Virac. Look for pili nut candies, dried laing, coconut vinegar, and woven abaca products. Buy pili directly from local processors for the freshest quality.
title: Where to Buy Pasalubong in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Pasalubong: Virac Public Market and main road shops. Pili nut candies, dried laing, coconut vinegar, abaca weavings.

input: What souvenirs should I buy in Catanduanes?
output: Top souvenirs: pili nut brittle and pastries, abaca weavings and bags, native coconut vinegar (sinamak), dried seafood (danggit, pusit), bottled laing. Buy at Virac Public Market for best prices.
title: Souvenirs from Catanduanes
summary_offline: Best souvenirs: pili nut brittle, abaca woven bags, coconut vinegar (sinamak), dried seafood, bottled laing. Best prices at Virac Public Market.

input: Where can I buy pili nuts in Catanduanes?
output: Virac Public Market has multiple stalls selling pili brittle, tarts, pastillas, and raw pili nuts. Some highway producers sell directly. Buy in bulk at the market — significantly cheaper than airport prices.
title: Pili Nuts Catanduanes
summary_offline: Buy pili nuts at Virac Public Market — brittle, tarts, pastillas, raw nuts. Much cheaper than Manila airport prices.

input: Is there a mall in Catanduanes?
output: There is no SM, Robinsons, or large shopping mall in Catanduanes. The main commercial area is Virac town proper with department stores, groceries, and pharmacies. Stock up on branded goods before arriving from Legazpi or Manila.
title: Mall in Catanduanes
summary_offline: No malls in Catanduanes. Shop at Virac town center. Stock up on branded goods before arriving.

input: Where can I buy groceries or supplies in Catanduanes?
output: Groceries available at Virac town center stores and Virac Public Market (freshest produce and seafood). Outside Virac, only small sari-sari stores. No 7-Eleven or Alfamart. Stock up in Virac before heading to remote areas.
title: Groceries in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Groceries: Virac town stores and Public Market. No 7-Eleven. Stock up in Virac before going to remote areas.

input: How big is Catanduanes island?
output: Catanduanes has a total land area of approximately 1,512 square kilometers. It is roughly 100 km from north to south and about 50 km wide. The island has 11 municipalities: Virac (capital), Bagamanoc, Baras, Bato, Caramoran, Gigmoto, Pandan, Panganiban, San Andres, San Miguel, and Viga.
title: Catanduanes Island Size
summary_offline: Catanduanes: 1,512 sq km. ~100 km long, 50 km wide. 11 municipalities including Virac (capital).

input: What direction is Catanduanes from Manila?
output: Catanduanes is located southeast of Manila, approximately 450 km away. It lies off the northeastern tip of the Bicol Peninsula in the Philippine Sea. Fly southeast to reach Virac, or travel by bus to Tabaco City then take a ferry eastward to Virac Port.
title: Catanduanes Direction from Manila
summary_offline: Catanduanes is ~450 km southeast of Manila. Fly Manila→Virac, or bus to Tabaco City then ferry east.

input: What is the climate like in Catanduanes? Is it always raining?
output: The dry season runs March to June — best time to visit. July to November is typhoon season with heavy rainfall and rough seas. December to February is cooler and can be rainy but typhoon risk drops. Despite its rain reputation, the dry months offer excellent weather.
title: Catanduanes Climate
summary_offline: Climate: March–June = dry season. July–Nov = typhoon season. Dec–Feb cooler and wetter but manageable. Best visit: March–June.

input: What is the emergency number in Catanduanes?
output: Dial 911 for the national emergency hotline. Catanduanes Provincial Hospital Virac: (052) 811-1260. PNP Virac: (052) 811-1335. Bureau of Fire Protection Virac: (052) 811-1034. Save these before arriving.
title: Emergency Numbers Catanduanes
summary_offline: Emergency: 911. Provincial Hospital Virac: (052) 811-1260. PNP: (052) 811-1335. BFP: (052) 811-1034.

input: Are there jellyfish in Catanduanes beaches?
output: Jellyfish are occasionally present when currents shift. Stings are usually minor. Rinse with seawater (not freshwater); apply vinegar for box jellyfish stings. Ask resort staff about current conditions before swimming.
title: Jellyfish in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Jellyfish occasionally present. Rinse with seawater + vinegar for box jellyfish. Ask resort staff about conditions.

input: What should I do if a typhoon hits while I am in Catanduanes?
output: Secure accommodation immediately, cancel all outdoor activities, monitor PAGASA (pagasa.dost.gov.ph), stay indoors away from windows. Flights and ferries will be suspended. Keep cash on hand — signal and power may go out.
title: What to Do During Typhoon in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Typhoon: book extra nights immediately, cancel outdoor trips, monitor PAGASA, stay indoors. Flights/ferries suspend. Keep cash.

input: Are there rip currents at Catanduanes beaches?
output: Yes, especially at Puraran Beach — a surfing beach with strong Pacific swells. Not for casual swimmers. Twin Rock Beach Resort and Mamangal Beach have calmer waters. Always ask locals about conditions.
title: Rip Currents in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Rip currents at Puraran — surf beach only. Safer: Twin Rock, Mamangal. Ask locals about conditions. Avoid open beaches in typhoon season.

input: Which network has signal in Catanduanes? Globe or Smart?
output: Both Globe and Smart have coverage, strongest in Virac and along main roads. Signal weakens in remote areas like Puraran Beach. Smart tends to be slightly better in mountainous eastern areas. Download offline maps before heading remote.
title: Mobile Network Coverage Catanduanes
summary_offline: Both Globe and Smart work in Virac and main towns. Signal weakens remotely. Download offline maps before going remote.

input: Are there power outages in Catanduanes?
output: Power outages (brownouts) are common, especially during typhoon season and in rural areas. Most hotels have generators. Bring a power bank — essential for remote stays like Puraran Beach.
title: Power Outages in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Power outages common in typhoon season and rural areas. Hotels usually have generators. Bring a power bank.

input: Can I use GCash in Catanduanes?
output: GCash works in Catanduanes with mobile data or WiFi. Some Virac establishments accept it for payment. However, many small businesses, habal-habal drivers, and market stalls are cash-only. ATMs (BDO, Landbank, Metrobank) are only in Virac.
title: GCash in Catanduanes
summary_offline: GCash works in Virac with signal. Many vendors are cash-only. ATMs only in Virac. Always carry PHP in small bills.

input: Are there any festivals in Catanduanes?
output: Main festival: Catandungan Festival in October — local dances, costumes, and traditions. Virac also celebrates its patron saint fiesta. Smaller barangay fiestas happen throughout the year. Book accommodation early during festivals.
title: Festivals in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Catandungan Festival (October) is the main festival. Virac patron saint fiesta + barangay fiestas year-round. Book early.

input: What is the history of Bato Church in Catanduanes?
output: Bato Church (Saint John the Baptist Parish) in Bato is one of the oldest Spanish colonial churches in Catanduanes, dating back to the 17th century. Built from local stone, it has survived typhoons and earthquakes and remains an active place of worship and cultural heritage site.
title: Bato Church History
summary_offline: Bato Church: 17th-century Spanish colonial stone church, still active. One of Catanduanes' oldest heritage sites.

input: What language do locals speak in Catanduanes? Will they understand me?
output: The primary local language is Viracnon (a Bicolano dialect) alongside Filipino. English is understood in Virac and among tourism workers. Basic Tagalog goes a long way in rural areas. Locals are generally warm and patient with visitors.
title: Language in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Local language: Viracnon + Filipino. English spoken in Virac. Basic Tagalog works everywhere. Locals are welcoming.

input: What is the local food in Catanduanes?
output: Local food is rooted in Bicolano cuisine. Must-try: laing (taro leaves in coconut cream), bicol express (pork with chili and coconut milk), sinantolan, fresh seafood (tanigue, kinilaw), pili nut desserts, and tinumok.
title: Local Food in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Must-try: laing, bicol express, sinantolan, fresh seafood, pili nut desserts, tinumok. Bicolano cuisine — generally spicy and coconut-milk based.

input: Where can I get breakfast in Virac?
output: Virac Public Market carinderias open from 4–5 AM serving rice, tuyo, and hot soup under PHP 100. Main road cafes open by 7 AM. Some hotels include breakfast.
title: Breakfast in Virac
summary_offline: Breakfast in Virac: Public Market carinderias open 4–5 AM (under PHP 100). Main road cafes open 7 AM. Some hotels include breakfast.

input: Are there vegetarian food options in Catanduanes?
output: Vegetarian options are limited but available. Laing is naturally vegetarian (ask if no shrimp paste). Fresh vegetables, rice, tofu, and fruit available at the public market. Bring snacks for remote areas.
title: Vegetarian Food in Catanduanes
summary_offline: Vegetarian: laing (ask if bagoong-free), vegetables, tofu, fruit. Public market is best source. Bring snacks for remote areas.

input: How far is Virac to Puraran Beach? How long does the drive take?
output: Puraran Beach is approximately 35–40 km from Virac, 1.5–2 hours by road. Van to Baras (PHP 80–120, ~1.5 hrs) then habal-habal to beach (PHP 80–120). Roads are winding.
title: Distance Virac to Puraran Beach
summary_offline: Virac to Puraran: ~35–40 km, 1.5–2 hrs. Van to Baras (PHP 80–120) then habal-habal (PHP 80–120). Winding roads.

input: How far is Virac to Binurong Point? How long is the drive?
output: Binurong Point is about 50–55 km from Virac, 2–3 hours by road. Most visitors hire a private van with driver (PHP 2,500–4,500 for the day). Road is scenic but winding and partially unpaved.
title: Distance Virac to Binurong Point
summary_offline: Virac to Binurong Point: ~50–55 km, 2–3 hrs. Best with private van (PHP 2,500–4,500/day). Winding, partially unpaved.

input: How far is Virac to Bato? How long does it take?
output: Bato is approximately 25–30 km south of Virac, 45 minutes to 1 hour by van (PHP 50–80). Hub for Maribina Falls, Nahulugan Falls, Palumbanes Island, and Bote Lighthouse.
title: Distance Virac to Bato
summary_offline: Virac to Bato: ~25–30 km, 45 min–1 hr by van (PHP 50–80). Hub for Maribina Falls, Nahulugan Falls, Palumbanes Island.

input: Ano ang pinakamagandang lugar sa Catanduanes?
output: Ang pinakamagandang lugar sa Catanduanes: Binurong Point (dramatic tanawin ng dagat), Puraran Beach (world-class surfing), Tuwad-Tuwadan Blue Lagoon (malinaw na tubig), Maribina Falls at Nahulugan Falls (magagandang talon), at Luyang Cave (cave exploration).
title: Pinakamagandang Lugar sa Catanduanes
summary_offline: Pinakamagandang lugar: Binurong Point, Puraran Beach, Tuwad-Tuwadan Lagoon, Maribina at Nahulugan Falls, Luyang Cave.

input: Magkano ang budget para sa isang araw sa Catanduanes?
output: Budget traveler: PHP 800–1,500/araw (pagkain, habal-habal, entrance fees). Mid-range: PHP 2,000–3,500 kasama accommodation at private transport. Dalhin ang cash — kakaunti ang GCash o card sa labas ng Virac.
title: Budget Para sa Catanduanes
summary_offline: Budget: PHP 800–1,500/araw (budget), PHP 2,000–3,500/araw (mid-range). Dalhin ang cash — limitado ang GCash sa labas ng Virac.

input: Paano pumunta sa Catanduanes mula Manila?
output: Via eroplano: Cebu Pacific o SkyJet mula NAIA papunta Virac Airport (VIC), mga 1 oras. Via ferry: bus/van to Tabaco City, Albay (10–12 oras) tapos RoRo ferry Tabaco-Virac (2–3 oras). Eroplano mas komportable; ferry mas mura.
title: Paano Pumunta sa Catanduanes mula Manila
summary_offline: Manila to Catanduanes: eroplano (1 oras) o bus to Tabaco + ferry (10–12 oras total). Eroplano mas komportable, ferry mas mura.

input: Is there a bar or restobar in Catanduanes?
output: Kuwago's Restobar is a popular bar and restaurant in Virac, Catanduanes, offering food and drinks in a relaxed setting.
title: Kuwago's Restobar
summary_offline: Kuwago's Restobar is a bar and restaurant in Virac serving food and drinks.

input: Where can I drink or hang out at night in Catanduanes?
output: DRUNKBOX is a bar in Virac, Catanduanes, known as a nightlife spot where locals and visitors hang out for drinks.
title: DRUNKBOX Bar
summary_offline: DRUNKBOX is a bar in Virac, Catanduanes popular for nightlife and drinks.

input: Is there a lounge or bar with a relaxed vibe in Virac?
output: Hideout Bar and Lounge is a chill bar and lounge in Virac, Catanduanes, offering a laid-back atmosphere for drinks and socializing.
title: Hideout Bar and Lounge
summary_offline: Hideout Bar and Lounge is a relaxed bar in Virac, Catanduanes for drinks and socializing.

input: Where can I eat and drink in Virac at night?
output: Kuwago's Restobar in Virac is a great spot for both food and drinks in the evening. It has a relaxed atmosphere popular with locals and tourists alike.
title: Kuwago's Restobar - Nighttime Dining
summary_offline: Kuwago's Restobar serves food and drinks at night in Virac.

input: What bars are open at night in Catanduanes?
output: Some bars open at night in Virac, Catanduanes include Kuwago's Restobar, DRUNKBOX, and Hideout Bar and Lounge. These are popular nightlife spots for locals and visitors.
title: Nightlife Bars in Virac
summary_offline: Kuwago's Restobar, DRUNKBOX, and Hideout Bar and Lounge are the main nightlife bars in Virac.

input: Where do locals drink in Catanduanes?
